# Frequently Asked Questions

A collection of common questions from MSc Geomatics students. Can't find your answer here? Ask on [Discord](https://discord.gg/XqNYUYCS7b) or contact us at <geomatics@tudelft.nl>.

* auto-generated ToC
{:toc}

---

## Before you start

**Q: Do I need to know how to code before starting?**

You don't need to be a programmer, but GEO1000 (Programming) moves fast and assumes you can pick things up quickly. If you've never written a line of code before, do a short Python intro over the summer — [Python for Everybody](https://www.py4e.com/) or the official Python tutorial are both fine. Students who arrive with zero experience tend to find Q1 stressful.


**Q: How much math do I actually need?**

Calculus and linear algebra come up repeatedly (DTM, 3D vision, machine learning). You don't need to be a mathematician, but if words like *eigenvalue* or *partial derivative* make you sweat, work through the TU Delft MOOCs before semester starts:

- [Calculus I](https://online-learning.tudelft.nl/courses/calculus-i-from-functions-to-differential-equations/) and [Calculus II](https://online-learning.tudelft.nl/courses/calculus-ii-multivariable-functions/)
- [Linear Algebra I](https://online-learning.tudelft.nl/courses/linear-algebra-i-vectors-and-linear-equations/) and [Linear Algebra II](https://online-learning.tudelft.nl/courses/linear-algebra-ii-matrices-and-linear-transformations/)
- [Probability Theory](https://online-learning.tudelft.nl/courses/probability-theory/)


They are free and genuinely useful.

---


## Programme & Courses

**Q: Where can I find the official study guide?**

The current study guide is available at [studyguide2526.tudelft.nl](https://studyguide2526.tudelft.nl/opleidingen/study-guide/educations/10542/). It lists all mandatory courses, electives, and graduation requirements.



**Q: Where do I find course materials and assignments?**

Most courses use [Brightspace](https://brightspace.tudelft.nl) for materials, assignments, and announcements. Some courses (e.g. GEO1015, GEO1016, GEO1004) have their own dedicated pages — check the links on the [home page](https://geomatics.bk.tudelft.nl/).



**Q: How do I register for exams?**

You must register for exams yourself through the student portal. Full instructions are on the [TU Delft exam registration page](https://www.tudelft.nl/en/student/my-study-me/education/courses-and-exams/exams/registration-for-exams). Missing the registration deadline means you cannot sit the exam.



**Q: Where can I find my timetable?**

Use [MyTimetable](https://mytimetable.tudelft.nl/schedule) to view your personal schedule. You can subscribe to it via your calendar app.

---

## Electives

**Q: How do I choose and register for an elective?**

See the dedicated [Electives page](https://geomatics.bk.tudelft.nl/electives/) for the full procedure. In short:

- **Pre-approved electives** — listed in the study guide, automatically added to OSIRIS once you pass.
- **Non-pre-approved courses** — you must request approval *before* the course starts by emailing <geomatics@tudelft.nl>.

---

**Q: When is the deadline to register for electives?**

For BK Faculty electives: end of **May** for Q1/Q2 courses, and early **December** for Q3/Q4 courses. Register through [BIS](https://bis.bk.tudelft.nl/student). Deadlines at other faculties may differ.

---

## Tools & Resources

**Q: Is there a student community or chat?**

Yes! Join the [MSc Geomatics Discord](https://discord.gg/XqNYUYCS7b) to connect with fellow students and ask questions.

---
**Q: How do I find housing?**

Start as early as possible — ideally the moment you receive your admission letter, which is often months before you arrive. The market is very competitive.

- [DUWO](https://www.duwo.nl/en/) is the main student housing provider in Delft. Register immediately, as waiting lists can be long.

---

## Administration & Forms

**Q: Who are the Board of Studies and Board of Examiners?**

- **Board of Studies (BoS)** — chaired by Martijn Meijers: handles curriculum and course matters. [More info](https://www.tudelft.nl/en/student/a-be-student-portal/organisation/boards-of-studies).
- **Board of Examiners (BoE)** — chaired by Hendrik Ploeger: handles exemptions, special cases, and approving non-standard electives. [More info](https://www.tudelft.nl/en/student/a-be-student-portal/organisation/board-of-examiners). Contact: <Examencommissie-BK@tudelft.nl>.

---

**Q: Who do I contact for academic advice or if I'm struggling?**

Reach out to the [Academic Counsellors](https://www.tudelft.nl/en/student/a-be-student-portal/organisation/academic-counsellors) at the faculty — they are there to help with personal circumstances, study delays, and more.

---

**Q: Where can I find the Student Charter and faculty regulations?**

- [Student Charter](https://www.tudelft.nl/en/student/a-be-student-portal/education/regulations/student-charter)
- [Faculty regulations](https://www.tudelft.nl/en/student/a-be-student-portal/education/regulations)
- [Fraud & Plagiarism policy](https://www.tudelft.nl/en/student/my-study-me/rules-guidelines-and-participation/fraud-plagiarism)

---

## Thesis

**Q: Which thesis course should I follow?**

- **[GEO2021](https://geomatics.bk.tudelft.nl/geo2021)** — for students starting their thesis in **2025 or later**.
- **[GEO2020](https://3d.bk.tudelft.nl/courses/geo2020)** — for students who started before 2025.

---

**Q: Where can I find thesis templates and writing tips?**

Templates and writing guidance are available on the [Geogeeks writing page](https://geomatics.bk.tudelft.nl/geogeeks/writing/).

---
